recommendation: Verify any harness-behavior claim against primary docs before freezing a design: this CR reopened its own frozen suite 3 times, all ratified, because round-1 wrongly assumed user-invocable:false/metadata.internal suppress model auto-invocation, and 'internal' collided with the pre-existing metadata.internal ('project-local internal') meaning. Recommend a producer pre-flight check -- confirm any asserted harness behavior against the harness's own docs, and grep the corpus for a term before coining a new category name -- to avoid costly multi-round re-opens on already-shipped scenarios.
evidence: 133-guard-internal-descriptions combat log, re-open #1: 'per Claude Code docs those do NOT suppress model auto-invocation' -- corrected after the initial design assumed otherwise
same log, re-open #2: 'internal' is overloaded -- metadata.internal:true is a distinct pre-existing 'project-local internal' category; renamed the new category to 'Partial Skill'
same log, re-open #3: corpus-wide propagation of the rename touched 2 more aced nodes, 6 subagent descriptions, and 3 website mirrors -- cost of not settling the term before the first freeze

recommendation: Reinforce two practices this mission's own gate history shows are load-bearing for governance/skill suites: (1) draw the control-flow graph and bind every edge 1:1 to a scenario -- that discipline is what surfaced a routing-overlap defect a sibling's earlier gate had only ever cleared 'by inspection', never by a blind routing run; recommend making CFG-drawn form + edge-binding a standard produce-time step, not an ad hoc one. (2) An atomicity/discrimination pair can be authored circularly (its own no-split branch justified by the same predicate it exists to test) and still pass a first cold-judge round; recommend the judge protocol add an explicit falsifiability check on a graded pair's own fixture before ALIGNED, since here it took a live implementation gap -- and a Council-ratified Oracle-lens reopen of a scenario this same mission had just frozen -- to surface it.
evidence: 304-m1-define-governance-suite.log.jsonl: 'atomicity-discriminator-circular' correction -- implementation clause justified its own no-split branch against the same predicate meant to test it; surfaced only because blind runs split the rule 2 of 3 times citing that clause

same log: a halt (oracle-lens-candidate) -- 'the failing scenario's own fixture is independently falsifiable ... a contract defect rather than an implementation defect' -- forced a Council-ratified unfreeze of a scenario this mission itself had frozen

same log: 'routing-overlap-on-the-improve-verb' -- a sibling skill's earlier gate had passed a routing scenario 'by inspection rather than by blind routing runs'; drawing the CFG and binding every edge to a scenario is what exposed that the earlier pass was false
